Rollins has walked a path to glory in WWE, adopting an innovative high-flying style that often leaves fans in awe as he executes his trademark Curb Stomp, ripping through opponents like a tornado. The Architect has amassed an impressive list of accomplishments, such as two-time WWE Universal Champion, two-time WWE Champion, and winning the 2019 Royal Rumble, proving to be the epitome of success in the squared circle. His intense rivalries with wrestlers like Dean Ambrose and Bray Wyatt have generated some of the most memorable moments in modern WWE history. Seth Rollins's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the 2019 Royal Rumble - Capturing the WWE Championship and the United States Championship at the same time in 2015 - Winning the NXT Championship in 2012 - Cashing in his Money in the Bank contract during WrestleMania 31 main event and winning the WWE World Heavyweight Championship What Makes CM Punk The Best Today: Known for his outspoken views and unapologetic attitude, CM Punk has consistently pushed boundaries both inside and outside the ring. His undeniable talent as a wrestler is only matched by his ability to captivate audiences with intense promos that showcase his intelligence and wit. With an impressive resume of championships under his belt, CM Punk continues to prove that he is a force to be reckoned with in the world of professional wrestling. CM Punk's Greatest Achievements of Their Career: - Winning the WWE Championship twice - Winning the WWE World Heavyweight Championship three times - Winning the Money in the Bank ladder match in 2008 and 2009 - Having one of the longest WWE Championship reigns in modern history

title: 10 Best Football Players To Ever Wrestle In WWE, Ranked
meta_description: These athletes didn't have much success in football, but did pretty well when they moved on o wrestling.
content: Many a wrestler has a previous background in football. Due to the size of the athletes and athleticism from football, making the transition to the world of wrestling a natural move. The likes of Brock Lesnar and Roman Reigns have made this transition seamlessly and are currently two of the top stars in WWE today. Whilst there have been a plethora of legends who also made the transition from the football field. Many have even played in the NFL and had a solid career before joining the WWE. So let's explore the best football players to ever wrestle inside a WWE ring. One of the most electrifying entertainers in wrestling history once had a career in football. Rock was viewed as a promising prospect as he signed with the University of Miami, however, he suffered numerous injuries. Related: 10 Football Players That Would Shine In Pro Wrestling This halted his football career as he signed on with the Calgary Stampeders for the Canadian Football League. He only lasted two months into the season before he was released and moved into pro wrestling. Baron Corbin plays a slimy heel currently in WWE. He is clearly highly thought of within the company due to his consistent TV time over the last few years. Before this, Corbin was a part of NXT after being signed up after his release from football. He was a part of Indianapolis Colts and Arizona Cardinals during a two-year spell from 2009 to 2011. This was after he impressed in the Divison II National Championships during his college days. This list is based on football ability rather than wrestling ability, otherwise, Rawley would not make this list. Rawley is widely known to be friends with Rob Gronkowski after playing with his brother during his college days. He was a part of the Green Bay Packers after impressing during a tryout in which he and two others were brought in from a group of 25. He also played for the Arizona Cardinals before an injury put him on the sidelines. Pillman was a fan favorite during his time in WWE, receiving huge pops any time he appeared in a wrestling ring. Sadly, Pillman could have been a megastar but passed away in 1997 aged 35. Before his wrestling career, he was apart of his hometown club Cincinnati Bengals. He impressed so much that he won the Ed Block Courage Award and was handed a contract for Buffalo Bills. Lesnar went down a different path than most on this list. Instead of moving from football to wrestling, he went the opposite way. Quitting WWE to try and realise his dream of playing in the NFL. He joined the Minnesota Viking after impressing during the NFL Combine. Lesnar appeared in many pre-season games and was offered the chance to represent the Vikings in NFL Europa but he turned it down and moved into MMA. During his college days, Reigns was voted Defensive Player Of The Year by Pensacola News Journal. He was eventually signed by Minnesota Vikings however he was shortly released after being diagnosed with leukaemia. Reigns recovered and joined Jacksonville Jaguars but again was released early but he did forge a career in the Canadian Football League. However, Reigns retired early and followed his family lineage into wrestling. Goldberg is one of the most intimidating men in wrestling history. His undefeated streak in WCW was iconic and he redeemed himself in WWE during his return feud with Brock Lesnar. He was drafted by NFL side LA Rams after impressing during his time in Georgia. Before moving on to Atlanta Falcons where Goldberg played 14 times in the NFL. However, his football career ended prematurely due to injury. A Hall Of Famer and multi-time champion within WWE, Ron Simmons wrestling career is a memorable one. However, his football career was also impressive as he was elected into the College Football Hall Of Fame after thriving for Florida State University. This resulted in him being snapped up by NFL side Cleveland Browns who he was with from 1981-82. Until he moved into the USFL with the Tampa Bay Bandits where he was teammates with Lex Luger. Vader is widely regarded as one of the most agile big men in wrestling history. He wrestled all over the US and Japan before finally coming to the WWE. Before his legendary wrestling career, he was a successful football player. Related: 10 Unbelievable Photos Of Current WWE Wrestlers Before They Were Stars He was signed by NFL side LA Rams and during his first season, he helped guide them to the Super Bowl earning an NFC Championship Ring in the process. However, he was forced to retire early due to injury Monty Brown is by far and away the best footballer to compete in a WWE ring. During his early football career, he was named MVP and Co-Player Of The Year for Ferris State Bulldogs. This resulted in him being signed by Buffalo Bills where he played professionally and competed in the Super Bowl. He then joined the New England Patriots, however, he only joined to be closer to the WWE Headquarters. Next: 10 WWE Superstars You Didn't Know Used To Play Football

The likes of Brock Lesnar and Roman Reigns have made this transition seamlessly and are currently two of the top stars in WWE today. Whilst there have been a plethora of legends who also made the transition from the football field. Many have even played in the NFL and had a solid career before joining the WWE. So let's explore the best football players to ever wrestle inside a WWE ring. One of the most electrifying entertainers in wrestling history once had a career in football. Rock was viewed as a promising prospect as he signed with the University of Miami, however, he suffered numerous injuries. Related: 10 Football Players That Would Shine In Pro Wrestling This halted his football career as he signed on with the Calgary Stampeders for the Canadian Football League. He only lasted two months into the season before he was released and moved into pro wrestling. Baron Corbin plays a slimy heel currently in WWE. He is clearly highly thought of within the company due to his consistent TV time over the last few years. Before this, Corbin was a part of NXT after being signed up after his release from football. He was a part of Indianapolis Colts and Arizona Cardinals during a two-year spell from 2009 to 2011. This was after he impressed in the Divison II National Championships during his college days. This list is based on football ability rather than wrestling ability, otherwise, Rawley would not make this list. Rawley is widely known to be friends with Rob Gronkowski after playing with his brother during his college days. He was a part of the Green Bay Packers after impressing during a tryout in which he and two others were brought in from a group of 25. He also played for the Arizona Cardinals before an injury put him on the sidelines. Pillman was a fan favorite during his time in WWE, receiving huge pops any time he appeared in a wrestling ring. Sadly, Pillman could have been a megastar but passed away in 1997 aged 35. Before his wrestling career, he was apart of his hometown club Cincinnati Bengals. He impressed so much that he won the Ed Block Courage Award and was handed a contract for Buffalo Bills. Lesnar went down a different path than most on this list. Instead of moving from football to wrestling, he went the opposite way. Quitting WWE to try and realise his dream of playing in the NFL. He joined the Minnesota Viking after impressing during the NFL Combine. Lesnar appeared in many pre-season games and was offered the chance to represent the Vikings in NFL Europa but he turned it down and moved into MMA. During his college days, Reigns was voted Defensive Player Of The Year by Pensacola News Journal. He was eventually signed by Minnesota Vikings however he was shortly released after being diagnosed with leukaemia. Reigns recovered and joined Jacksonville Jaguars but again was released early but he did forge a career in the Canadian Football League. However, Reigns retired early and followed his family lineage into wrestling. Goldberg is one of the most intimidating men in wrestling history. His undefeated streak in WCW was iconic and he redeemed himself in WWE during his return feud with Brock Lesnar. He was drafted by NFL side LA Rams after impressing during his time in Georgia. Before moving on to Atlanta Falcons where Goldberg played 14 times in the NFL. However, his football career ended prematurely due to injury. A Hall Of Famer and multi-time champion within WWE, Ron Simmons wrestling career is a memorable one. However, his football career was also impressive as he was elected into the College Football Hall Of Fame after thriving for Florida State University. This resulted in him being snapped up by NFL side Cleveland Browns who he was with from 1981-82. Until he moved into the USFL with the Tampa Bay Bandits where he was teammates with Lex Luger. Vader is widely regarded as one of the most agile big men in wrestling history. He wrestled all over the US and Japan before finally coming to the WWE. Before his legendary wrestling career, he was a successful football player. Related: 10 Unbelievable Photos Of Current WWE Wrestlers Before They Were Stars He was signed by NFL side LA Rams and during his first season, he helped guide them to the Super Bowl earning an NFC Championship Ring in the process. However, he was forced to retire early due to injury Monty Brown is by far and away the best footballer to compete in a WWE ring. During his early football career, he was named MVP and Co-Player Of The Year for Ferris State Bulldogs. This resulted in him being signed by Buffalo Bills where he played professionally and competed in the Super Bowl. He then joined the New England Patriots, however, he only joined to be closer to the WWE Headquarters. Next: 10 WWE Superstars You Didn't Know Used To Play Football

content: UFC Fight Night hits London, England with a card headlined by Movsar Evloev versus Lerone Murphy at the O2 Arena. On the night of a wild Drew McIntyre vs. Jacob Fatu brawl, here are three things we hated & three things we loved about the 3/20/26 episode of "WWE SmackDown!" This is Wrestling Inc.’s results for “WWE SmackDown” on March 20, 2026, with the WWE Tag Team & Women's Tag Team Championships up for grabs! Another AEW star is nearing a return from injury as they were backstage for "AEW Dynamite" and "AEW Collision." Former two-time WWE Tag Team Champion Tama Tonga is in hot water after posting a rehearsal video from last week's "WWE SmackDown." WWE held a discount sale on March 16 for a number of shows including WrestleMania 42, and looking at the figures, it looks to have worked well. Bayley reveals that she despised her theme song as the Hugger, but chose to keep it after discovering that management loved it. WWE star Chelsea Green opens up about her approaches to her two reigns as Women's United States Champion. EVIL looks all but set to join WWE in the near future, but the former NJPW star wants to start in "WWE NXT" rather than the main roster. Former founding member of the Midnight Express, Dennis Condrey, passed away Friday evening at 74. AEW's Dax Harwood creates GoFundMe page for his late idol. "The Most Dangerous Man in AEW" Swerve Strickland said that evolution is a key part to his wrestling persona, especially over the last four years in AEW. WWE Hall of Famer and AEW commentator Jim Ross doesn't believe that fellow Hall of Famer Stone Cold Steve Austin will ever wrestle a match again Billy Gunn reflects on upsetting Vince McMahon in what he considers the most hilarious moment of his career in WWE. David Sahadi has discussed his WWE exit in the early 2000s and how he believes Kevin Dunn negatively influenced his relationship with Vince McMahon. WWE HOFer JBL doesn't think he'd be where he is, if not for Ron SImmons. WWE Hall of Famer "Stone Cold" Steve Austin used to get fired up over one thing about Vince McMahon whenever they shared the ring. AEW Women's World Champion Thekla explains the mutual benefits of her partnership with Triangle of Madness stablemates Julia Hart and Skye Blue AEW broadcaster Tony Schiavone recently highlighted one performer the company has under contract that he feels is not featured enough. The March 18 episode of "AEW Dynamite" saw the fallout from AEW Revolution, and there was a lot of buzz in the TV ratings. The Bella Twins saw their first shot at the Women's Tag Team Championships end in disqualification during "WWE SmackDown." Carmelo Hayes defeated Ilja Dragunov for the Men's US title last December. Michin and B-Fab revealed on "WWE SmackDown" that they now have Jade Cargill's back, and beat down her WrestleMania 42 opponent, Rhea Ripley, in the ring. UFC announced it will be working with the Association of Boxing Commissions for the White House card, though the DC Combat Sports Commission are not impressed. President Donald Trump has been promoting the UFC's upcoming White House event, Freedom 250, with many within his Republican circle clamoring for tickets. Cody Rhodes is not angry with rapper Travis Scott for the slap he landed on him in 2025, claiming that it wasn’t the hardest slap he has received in WWE. Damian Priest and R-Truth dethroned the MFTs to win the WWE Tag Team Championship on "WWE SmackDown." Tyson Fury has joined the growing list of heavyweights interested in a bout with Jon Jones. According to a report, at least one advertised match on "WWE SmackDown" is no longer scheduled to take place. The Bella Twins discussed their match for the WWE Women's Tag Team Championships tonight on "WWE SmackDown" in length on the "Nikki & Brie Show." Paul "Triple H" Levesque announced that Nickelback's latest song, their first since 2022, will be an official theme of WrestleMania 42.

title: List of WWE personnel - Wikipedia
content: WWE is an American entertainment company and professional wrestling promotion based in Stamford, Connecticut.[1] It is owned and operated by TKO Group Holdings, itself a majority owned subsidiary of Endeavor Group Holdings. WWE personnel consists of professional wrestlers, managers, play-by-play and color commentators, ring announcers, interviewers, referees, trainers, producers, script writers, and various other positions. Executives and board members are also listed. As of 2024, WWE employs over 800 full-time employees as part of its business operations, spread across its three segments: Media, Live Events, and Consumer Products. In addition, they contract with various other vendors and service providers.[2] As part of its Live Events segment, WWE signs performers known as professional wrestlers; these performers are described by the company as "independent contractors",[1] with contracts for performers typically ranging from short developmental to multi-year deals.[3][4] Since the reintroduction of the WWE brand extension in 2016, performers are assigned to one of two primary "main roster" brands: Raw and SmackDown. A third brand, NXT, had originally been designated as a developmental brand for the main roster. In 2019, it was elevated to the third main roster brand.[5][6][7] In 2021, the brand's weekly television show was rebooted as "NXT 2.0", and was reinstated to its original status as the developmental division for the main roster (Raw and SmackDown), although it has since dropped the "2.0" name.[8] In 2025, WWE introduced a fourth brand, called Evolve, which features wrestlers who are just beginning their training at the WWE Performance Center under the WWE NIL program, as well as wrestlers in independent promotions that are part of the WWE ID program, with the goal of advancing to NXT.[9] Main roster performers primarily appear on Monday Night Raw and Friday Night SmackDown. Advanced developmental wrestlers appear on NXT, while beginning developmental wrestlers appear on Evolve. Supplementary shows include Main Event, which features Raw and SmackDown wrestlers (with occasional appearances from NXT wrestlers), and LFG, which features Evolve wrestlers. Performers may also appear on other WWE weekly television programming, as well as on its Premium Live Events and untelevised house shows. A majority of personnel in its developmental system train at the WWE Performance Center in Orlando, Florida. For the most part, the brands have their own distinct championships, although some select titles are open to all the brands, such as the WWE Speed Championship for male wrestlers, as well as the WWE Women's Speed Championship and WWE Women's Tag Team Championship for female wrestlers. WWE wrestlers holding championships from Mexican sister promotion Lucha Libre AAA Worldwide (AAA), U.S. partner promotion Total Nonstop Action Wrestling (TNA), and Japanese partner promotion Pro Wrestling Noah (Noah) are also recognized by WWE. WWE refers to talent not specifically assigned to a brand as "free agents", who may appear on any show.[174] There are several announcers who cover WWE broadcasts. This group includes backstage interviewers, presenters, ringside commentators, ring announcers, pre-show hosts, and analysts. Select former or retired WWE wrestlers not on the main roster (sometimes billed as "WWE Legends") and other celebrities and personalities have contracts to make periodic appearances—either on WWE TV or for promotional events—serving as "ambassadors" (public representatives/spokespeople) for the company. As both NXT and Evolve are based at the Performance Center (PC), PC coaches and trainers also work as producers for those brands.

WWE Smackdown Live, March 20th, 2026, Watch-Wrestling FHD stream The Road to WrestleMania 42 intensifies as Friday Night SmackDown heads to the Lenovo Center in Raleigh, North Carolina, just one month before the “Showcase of the Immortals.” All eyes are on 14-time World Champion Randy Orton, who will finally break his silence following his brutal betrayal of WWE Champion Cody Rhodes. During last week’s WrestleMania contract signing, Orton savagely attacked his longtime friend and former protégé, leaving the American Nightmare battered. On Monday Night Raw, Orton declared himself a “killer,” hinting at a reminder of his ruthless roots and taking shots at wrestling’s royal families while addressing Michael Cole. Fans eagerly await his explanation for turning on the man he once called a brother. The night promises high-stakes action beyond Orton’s appearance. Fresh off winning a grueling Tag Team Turmoil Match to earn No. 1 contender status, Damian Priest and R-Truth challenge the dominant WWE Tag Team Champions, The MFTs, in a blockbuster title bout. Adding to the excitement, WWE Hall of Famers The Bella Twins—Nikki and Brie—make their return, gunning for the WWE Women’s Tag Team Championship. They’ll face powerhouse duo Nia Jax and Lash Legend, aiming to reclaim glory in the division. Watch SmackDown this Friday at 8 p.m. ET/7 CT on SmackDown on USA.
